title: Cover / title page
role: Set the tone for the whole book: serious, beautiful, first-principles, transformation-centered, adult, and visually irresistible without becoming sci-fi wallpaper.
prompt_core: Create a cover illustration for an adult mathematics book that begins with a single segment and unfolds through arrow, circle, graph, complex plane, and spacetime grid into a coherent transformation-based cosmos. The composition should feel like mathematics growing from concrete geometry into modern abstraction without ever breaking its spine. Rich but disciplined, elegant typography space at top and center, no embedded text, no classroom cliché, no random equations floating in fog.
caption: From segment to spacetime, one scaffold.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.
api_prompt:
Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

Project context:
This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "Cover / title page Cover / title page".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Set the tone for the whole book: serious, beautiful, first-principles, transformation-centered, adult, and visually irresistible without becoming sci-fi wallpaper.

Layout intent:
F — Full-page opener

Style direction:
synthesis cover art

Core image brief:
Create a cover illustration for an adult mathematics book that begins with a single segment and unfolds through arrow, circle, graph, complex plane, and spacetime grid into a coherent transformation-based cosmos. The composition should feel like mathematics growing from concrete geometry into modern abstraction without ever breaking its spine. Rich but disciplined, elegant typography space at top and center, no embedded text, no classroom cliché, no random equations floating in fog.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
From segment to spacetime, one scaffold.

Overlay / production note:
No mandatory overlay note.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

source_key: Front matter opener: school made mathematics feel fake
title: Front matter opener: school made mathematics feel fake
role: Acknowledge the reader’s experience without pity and signal that this book rebuilds trust.
prompt_core: Show a sterile worksheet-like world of disconnected symbols fading away while, in the foreground, real geometric objects and transformations—sticks, arrows, circles, curves—re-enter as tangible, intelligible things. The mood should be relief and re-entry into meaning, not therapy cliché. Adult, dignified, low-drama, intellectually welcoming.
caption: This book returns mathematics to things you can see and act on.

source_key: Decoding tool opener: picture, action, relation, compression
title: Decoding tool opener: picture, action, relation, compression
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Give the reader the four-lens method in one memorable image.
prompt_core: Design a four-quadrant image around one mathematical idea—such as addition or derivative—shown as picture, action, relation, and compression. Each quadrant should feel different in medium but clearly belong to the same concept: a drawn scene, a transformation process, an invariant relationship, and a symbolic packaging. Elegant, didactic without being textbooky, overlay-friendly for labels later.
caption: Every new idea can be decoded four ways.

title: Permanent scaffold plate
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
role: Give the whole document a recurring visual anchor that readers can return to.
prompt_core: Create a master visual atlas linking objects, sameness relations, operations, and invariants across the whole book. Segments, directed arrows, triangles, curves, functions, vectors, and spacetime intervals should appear as stations in one transformation-based system. The image should be rich enough to revisit throughout the book but not crowded. Elegant editorial map, adult palette, room for later labels.

title: Roadmap of the journey
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
role: Make the five-part structure feel like a navigable expedition rather than a syllabus.
prompt_core: Design a five-part journey map that begins with raw magnitudes and ends in modern mathematical landscapes. Each part should have its own terrain character—workshop, instrument room, celestial chamber, analytic city, spacetime atlas—while remaining connected by a single path of transformations and invariants. The tone should be exploratory and rigorous, not fantasy-cartoon.
caption: This is a journey through one evolving language, not a pile of disconnected units.

title: Part II opener
role: Announce operations as transformations rather than arithmetic recipes.
prompt_core: Create a Part II opener in which arrows, scaling rays, mirrors, and ratio machines appear as a family of operations acting on a common geometric world. The image should feel like entering a room full of instruments that do mathematics by moving, resizing, reversing, and composing. Elegant, kinetic, no clutter.

title: Appendix A timeline
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Give the historical timeline a clean visual spine so it feels like living development rather than trivia.
prompt_core: Design a horizontally flowing timeline mural that moves from ancient geometry and ratio crises through analytic geometry, calculus, complex numbers, linear algebra, relativity, and modern branches. Use a restrained icon system and evolving visual motifs rather than portraits alone. Clean, elegant, highly navigable.
caption: The subject grew by answering pressure, not by accreting random facts.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.
api_prompt:
Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

Project context:
This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "Appendix A timeline Appendix A timeline".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Give the historical timeline a clean visual spine so it feels like living development rather than trivia.

Layout intent:
D — Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed

Style direction:
timeline mural

Core image brief:
Design a horizontally flowing timeline mural that moves from ancient geometry and ratio crises through analytic geometry, calculus, complex numbers, linear algebra, relativity, and modern branches. Use a restrained icon system and evolving visual motifs rather than portraits alone. Clean, elegant, highly navigable.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
The subject grew by answering pressure, not by accreting random facts.

Overlay / production note:
No mandatory overlay note.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

title: Appendix D proof-reading guide
role: Turn proof-reading from dread into a navigable practice.
prompt_core: Create an image of a proof as a path through a structured landscape: assumptions at one end, allowed moves as stepping stones, conclusions at the far side, and common wrong turns faintly visible but not dominant. The image should be calming and procedural without becoming childish. It should tell the reader that a proof is a route to be walked, not a wall to be feared.
caption: A proof is a path of allowed moves, not a performance of brilliance.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame.
